meaning: n.复发,回复原状vi.故态复萌,旧病复发
examples: In 90 percent of cases the patient will relapse within six months.
You are well now, but mind you don't have a relapse.
He handed in his resignation at once—and that night the Judge suffered a relapse and died.
It is now realized that some children do go into remission and may relapse later.
People who taper off the medication too quickly are more likely to have a relapse.
